chief_complaint: A 45-year-old male presented with fever, cough, retrosternal pain, dyspnea, and nausea accompanied by vomiting.
history: The patient's retrosternal pain was persistent and nonradiating and was aggravated by coughing. He was initially suspected to have viral myocarditis due to the combination of retrosternal pain, fever, and dyspnea.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 38.5°C, respiratory rate 24/min, oxygen saturation 88% on room air. Physical examination revealed diffuse tracheobronchial ulcerations on bronchoscopy.
labs: Bronchoscopy revealed diffuse tracheobronchial ulcerations. Imaging and other tests were not mentioned in the case report.
correct_diagnosis: Tracheobronchial ulcers and invasive pulmonary aspergillosis caused by Aspergillus infection
differentials: Tracheobronchial ulcers and invasive pulmonary aspergillosis caused by Aspergillus infection
correct_ranking: Tracheobronchial ulcers and invasive pulmonary aspergillosis caused by Aspergillus infection
